reviews.review_title: Great for learning Chess, Shogi, Xiangqi
reviews.review_description:
We’ve been using VRShogiMates in our school strategy games CCA to introduce students to Chess, Xiangqi, and Shogi, and it has worked very well. Many of our learners already play games like chess or Xiangqi, but Shogi and some other strategy games can feel a bit complicated at first.
(especially Chu Shogi) 
The VR environment makes the board much easier to understand and keeps learners engaged, even if they are completely new to the game.
The controls can take a little time to learn for first-time VR users, but once students get used to them, the experience becomes smooth.

Overall, it’s a great way to explore strategic board games in an interactive environment. Highly recommended for schools, clubs, and beginners who want to learn the game.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for using VRShogiMates with your school CCA! It’s great to hear that learners are exploring Chess, Xiangqi, and Shogi together. One of the goals of the app is to make complex strategy games easier to understand in VR, including variants like Chu Shogi.
If your learners enjoy exploring new strategy games, they might also like trying Gungi.
And yes, VR controls can take a little getting used to at first, but once players are familiar with them the experience becomes quite smooth. Thanks again for sharing your experience!

reviews.review_title: Interesting idea, could use more polish
reviews.review_description:
I tried VRShogiMates because I enjoy strategy games like chess and Xiangqi and was curious about Shogi. The VR setting actually makes it easier to see the board and understand what’s going on, which helps when learning a game that can feel quite complicated at first. That said, the app doesn’t feel perfectly polished yet. The controls take a bit of time to get used to, and there isn’t much of a tutorial to guide new players through the basics. If you already know a little about Shogi it’s easier to jump in, but complete beginners might need to look things up elsewhere first. You also need another player to play against
Still, the core idea is good and it’s nice to see a traditional board game explored in VR. I’m hoping future updates add a proper tutorial and refine some of the controls, because the concept definitely has potential.

Gave VRShogiMates a try since I’m into board games like chess, and I’ve always been a bit curious about Shogi. The VR part is pretty cool and definitely makes the game feel more interesting than just looking at a board on a screen.

But honestly, it feels a bit unfinished right now. The controls are not super intuitive at first, and there’s basically no real tutorial, so you’re kind of left figuring things out on your own. If you don’t already know how Shogi works, it can be confusing. The other games do look interesting, but couldn't wrap my head around them

It’s not bad, just not something I’d recommend to complete beginners yet. If they add a proper tutorial and smooth things out a bit, this could turn into something really solid.

reviews.review_title: Tricky controls, but a great experience
reviews.review_description:
Got the Quest version, played with a friend, then messaged the dev on discord and got a free Steam key. 

This game throws you into a VR dojo with a bunch of classic board games—Shogi, Chess, Xiangqi, even Gungi (which I hadn’t heard of before and now fear slightly). It’s all beautifully done, but the controls take a bit of getting used to. Pro tip: the YouTube tutorial helps a lot.

Gungi is wild. It feels like Chess and Shogi had a brilliant but slightly unhinged child.

If you’re into strategy games and don’t mind a learning curve, this is a solid pick—especially considering it’s made by one guy. Respect.

Recommended. Just be ready to spend the first few matches losing to your own hands.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the great review and for giving the games a try with a friend!
Glad the tutorial helped. The controls can take a little time to get used to in VR, but once it clicks it usually feels quite natural.
And yes, Gungi has that effect on people. “Chess and Shogi’s slightly unhinged child” might be one of the best descriptions I’ve heard.
Really appreciate the support and kind words!

reviews.review_description:
Overall, definitely worth it if you like any (or all) of the chess-like board games. It isn't the most polished game I've played, and it's missing a computer to play against. BUT, it literally lets me play a game of chess in real time with my brother who lives across the country. For the Hunter x Hunter fans out there, I can play actual Gungi !! (a next level multi tiered chess/shogi strategy game) with my friend. Correspondence mode (async playing) is also super sweet, since sometimes I don't have the time to sit down for a full game. Instead, I can send one move every day or two and play a game with friends over the course of a month. 

The controls aren't super intuitive, but there is a great instruction panel, so it's worth reading it. Also, there is a bunch of content for people to learn about the various games, so that's sweet. The more I've played around with the different games, the more cool little things I discover. 

While the appearance might not "Wow" you at first, the actual functionality of the game and possibilities it opens for chess, shogi, gungi, and the others (yes, there's more lol) is awesome and makes getting this app worth it IMHO.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the thoughtful review! Really glad you're enjoying the ability to play with friends and family, especially across long distances. That was one of the main ideas behind the project.
Great to hear you're exploring different games as well. Gungi definitely surprises a lot of people once they discover it.
And thanks for mentioning the instructions. VR controls can take a bit of getting used to at first, but they usually become much more natural after a few games.
Appreciate the support and hope you and your friends enjoy many more matches!

reviews.review_title: Great for shogi and chess enthusiasts
reviews.review_description: The game is fun and works great on my Quest 2. I love the authentic Japanese setting and the way the board and the pieces look and sound. The game provides a calming experience when analyzing a position, while it also offers excitement as the only way to challenge an opponent to a shogi game in VR.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the kind review! I’m really glad you’re enjoying the atmosphere and the board design. Creating a calm place where players can focus on the game was very much the goal.
And yes, being able to sit across from someone and play Shogi in VR is a special experience. Really appreciate the support, and hope you enjoy many more matches!
